title: Feedback Loop Explorer
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Side-by-side animated comparison of reinforcing and balancing feedback loops with real-time graphs across multiple ecological scenarios

learningObjectives: Differentiate between reinforcing and balancing feedback loops and predict their effects on system behavior
Identify reinforcing and balancing loops in real ecological scenarios
Explain how the interaction of both loop types determines ecosystem stability
prerequisites: Basic understanding of cause and effect in ecological systems
Ability to read and interpret line graphs

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Side-by-side comparison with multiple scenario selection
keyQuestions: What shape does the graph take for a reinforcing loop vs. a balancing loop?
Why do 'positive' and 'negative' feedback not mean 'good' and 'bad'?
What happens when a reinforcing loop overpowers a balancing loop in an ecosystem?
Can you identify both loop types in a single ecological system?
commonMisconceptions: Students frequently assume 'positive feedback' means a good outcome and 'negative feedback' means a bad outcome
Students may think balancing loops always produce a perfectly flat line rather than oscillation
Students may not realize that most real ecosystems contain both reinforcing and balancing loops simultaneously
assessmentOpportunities: Students draw and label causal loop diagrams for each scenario
Students predict graph shapes before viewing simulation output
Students identify both loop types in a real-world ecological issue and create diagrams
